#### apps/desktop/electron/vscode-marketplace.cjs — calls(calls), request(function), request(calls)

```javascript
26	const ID_RE = /^[\w-]+\.[\w-]+$/
27	
28	/** Minimal HTTPS helper with redirect-following, timeout, and a size cap. */
29	function request(url, { method = 'GET', headers = {}, body = null, maxBytes = MAX_VSIX_BYTES } = {}, redirectsLeft = MAX_REDIRECTS) {
30	  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
31	    const req = https.request(url, { method, headers }, res => {
32	      const status = res.statusCode ?? 0
33	
34	      if (status >= 300 && status < 400 && res.headers.location) {
35	        if (redirectsLeft <= 0) {
36	          res.resume()
37	          reject(new Error('Too many redirects.'))
38	
39	          return
40	        }
41	
42	        const next = new URL(res.headers.location, url).toString()
43	        res.resume()
44	        // Redirects to the CDN are plain GETs (drop the POST body).
45	        resolve(request(next, { method: 'GET', headers: { 'User-Agent': headers['User-Agent'] }, maxBytes }, redirectsLeft - 1))
46	
47	        return
48	      }
49	
50	      if (status < 200 || status >= 300) {
51	        res.resume()
52	        reject(new Error(`Request failed (${status}) for ${url}`))
53	
54	        return
55	      }
56	
57	      const chunks = []
58	      let total = 0
59	
60	      res.on('data', chunk => {
61	        total += chunk.length
62	
63	        if (total > maxBytes) {
64	          req.destroy()
65	          reject(new Error('Response exceeded the size limit.'))
66	
67	          return
68	        }
69	
70	        chunks.push(chunk)
71	      })
72	      res.on('end', () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)))
73	    })
74	
75	    req.on('error', reject)
76	    req.setTimeout(REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS, () => req.destroy(new Error('Request timed out.')))
77	
78	    if (body) {
79	      req.write(body)
80	    }
81	
82	    req.end()
83	  })
84	}
85	
86	/** Resolve `{ displayName, vsixUrl }` for the latest version of `id`. */
87	async function resolveExtension(id) {
```

#### gateway/platforms/base.py — is_command(calls), MessageEvent(class), is_command(method), get_command(method), get_command_args(method), calls(calls)

```python
1420	
1421	
1422	@dataclass
1423	class MessageEvent:
1424	    """
1425	    Incoming message from a platform.
1426	    
1427	    Normalized representation that all adapters produce.
1428	    """
1429	    # Message content
1430	    text: str
1431	    message_type: MessageType = MessageType.TEXT
1432	    
1433	    # Source information
1434	    source: SessionSource = None
1435	    
1436	    # Original platform data
1437	    raw_message: Any = None
1438	    message_id: Optional[str] = None
1439	
1440	    # Platform-specific update identifier.  For Telegram this is the
1441	    # ``update_id`` from the PTB Update wrapper; other platforms currently
1442	    # ignore it.  Used by ``/restart`` to record the triggering update so the
1443	    # new gateway can advance the Telegram offset past it and avoid processing
1444	    # the same ``/restart`` twice if PTB's graceful-shutdown ACK times out
1445	    # ("Error while calling `get_updates` one more time to mark all fetched
1446	    # updates" in gateway.log).
1447	    platform_update_id: Optional[int] = None
1448	    
1449	    # Media attachments
1450	    # media_urls: local file paths (for vision tool access)
1451	    media_urls: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
1452	    media_types: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
1453	    
1454	    # Reply context
1455	    reply_to_message_id: Optional[str] = None
1456	    reply_to_text: Optional[str] = None  # Text of the replied-to message (for context injection)
1457	    
1458	    # Auto-loaded skill(s) for topic/channel bindings (e.g., Telegram DM Topics,
1459	    # Discord channel_skill_bindings).  A single name or ordered list.
1460	    auto_skill: Optional[str | list[str]] = None
1461	
1462	    # Per-channel ephemeral system prompt (e.g. Discord channel_prompts).
1463	    # Applied at API call time and never persisted to transcript history.
1464	    channel_prompt: Optional[str] = None
1465	
1466	    # Channel context recovered by history backfill (e.g. messages between
1467	    # bot turns that were missed due to require_mention).  Kept separate
1468	    # from ``text`` so the sender-prefix logic in run.py can operate on the
1469	    # trigger message alone, then prepend this context afterward.
1470	    channel_context: Optional[str] = None
1471	    
1472	    # Internal flag — set for synthetic events (e.g. background process
1473	    # completion notifications) that must bypass user authorization checks.
1474	    internal: bool = False
1475	
1476	    # Timestamps
1477	    timestamp: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.now)
1478	    
1479	    def is_command(self) -> bool:
1480	        """Check if this is a command message (e.g., /new, /reset)."""
1481	        return self.text.startswith("/")
1482	    
1483	    def get_command(self) -> Optional[str]:
1484	        """Extract command name if this is a command message."""
1485	        if not self.is_command():
1486	            return None
1487	        # Split on space and get first word, strip the /
1488	        parts = self.text.split(maxsplit=1)
1489	        raw = parts[0][1:].lower() if parts else None
1490	        if raw and "@" in raw:
1491	            raw = raw.split("@", 1)[0]
1492	        # Reject file paths: valid command names never contain /
1493	        if raw and "/" in raw:
1494	            return None
1495	        return raw
1496	    
1497	    def get_command_args(self) -> str:
1498	        """Get the arguments after a command."""
1499	        if not self.is_command():
1500	            return self.text
1501	        parts = self.text.split(maxsplit=1)
1502	        args = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""
1503	        # iOS auto-corrects -- to — (em dash) and - to – (en dash)
1504	        args = args.replace("\u2014\u2014", "--").replace("\u2014", "--").replace("\u2013", "-")
1505	        return args
1506	
1507	
1508	@dataclass
```

#### apps/desktop/src/components/assistant-ui/user-message-text.tsx — UserMessageTextProps(interface)

```tsx
1	import type { FC } from 'react'
2	import { Fragment, useMemo } from 'react'
3	
4	import { DirectiveContent } from '@/components/assistant-ui/directive-text'
5	import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'
6	
7	// User messages should render the bare-minimum of markdown: backtick `code`
8	// spans and ``` fenced blocks. We deliberately don't pull in the full
9	// assistant Markdown pipeline (Streamdown + KaTeX + syntax highlighter)
10	// because user input rarely contains structured docs and the heavy pipeline
11	// adds a lot of runtime cost per bubble.
12	//
13	// Directive chips (`@file:`, `@image:`, ...) still resolve via DirectiveContent
14	// inside the plain-text segments.
15	
16	interface FenceSegment {
17	  kind: 'fence'
18	  code: string
19	  lang: string | null
20	}
21	
22	interface InlineSegment {
23	  kind: 'inline'
24	  text: string
25	}
26	
27	interface InlineCodeSegment {
28	  kind: 'inline-code'
29	  code: string
30	}
31	
32	interface InlineTextSegment {
33	  kind: 'inline-text'
34	  text: string
35	}
36	
37	type TopSegment = FenceSegment | InlineSegment
38	type InlineNode = InlineCodeSegment | InlineTextSegment
39	
40	const FENCE_RE = /```([^\n`]*)\n([\s\S]*?)```/g
41	
42	// Greedy backtick run length so ``code with `backticks` inside`` works.
43	const INLINE_CODE_RE = /(`+)([^`\n][\s\S]*?)\1/g
44	
45	function splitFences(text: string): TopSegment[] {
46	  const segments: TopSegment[] = []
47	  let cursor = 0
48	
49	  for (const match of text.matchAll(FENCE_RE)) {
50	    const start = match.index ?? 0
51	
52	    if (start > cursor) {
53	      segments.push({ kind: 'inline', text: text.slice(cursor, start) })
54	    }
55	
56	    segments.push({
57	      kind: 'fence',
58	      lang: (match[1] || '').trim() || null,
59	      code: match[2] ?? ''
60	    })
61	    cursor = start + match[0].length
62	  }
63	
64	  if (cursor < text.length) {
65	    segments.push({ kind: 'inline', text: text.slice(cursor) })
66	  }
67	
68	  return segments
69	}
70	
71	function splitInlineCode(text: string): InlineNode[] {
72	  const nodes: InlineNode[] = []
73	  let cursor = 0
74	
75	  for (const match of text.matchAll(INLINE_CODE_RE)) {
76	    const start = match.index ?? 0
77	
78	    if (start > cursor) {
79	      nodes.push({ kind: 'inline-text', text: text.slice(cursor, start) })
80	    }
81	
82	    nodes.push({ kind: 'inline-code', code: match[2] })
83	    cursor = start + match[0].length
84	  }
85	
86	  if (cursor < text.length) {
87	    nodes.push({ kind: 'inline-text', text: text.slice(cursor) })
88	  }
89	
90	  return nodes
91	}
92	
93	interface UserMessageTextProps {
94	  text: string
95	  className?: string
96	}
97	
98	export const UserMessageText: FC<UserMessageTextProps> = ({ className, text }) => {
99	  const top = useMemo(() => splitFences(text), [text])
100	
101	  return (
102	    <span className={cn('block', className)} data-slot="aui_user-message-text">
103	      {top.map((segment, segmentIndex) => {
104	        if (segment.kind === 'fence') {
105	          return (
106	            <pre
107	              className="my-1.5 max-w-full overflow-x-auto rounded-md border border-border/45 bg-[color-mix(in_srgb,currentColor_5%,transparent)] px-2.5 py-2 font-mono text-[0.86em] leading-snug"
108	              data-slot="aui_user-fence"
109	              key={`fence-${segmentIndex}`}
110	            >
111	              <code className="block whitespace-pre">{segment.code}</code>
112	            </pre>
113	          )
114	        }
115	
116	        return (
117	          <Fragment key={`inline-${segmentIndex}`}>
118	            <InlineSegmentView text={segment.text} />
119	          </Fragment>
120	        )
121	      })}
122	    </span>
123	  )
124	}
125	
126	const InlineSegmentView: FC<{ text: string }> = ({ text }) => {
127	  const nodes = useMemo(() => splitInlineCode(text), [text])
128	
129	  return (
130	    // styles.css bidi hook (#44150); whitespace-pre-line makes each line its own
131	    // UAX#9 paragraph so it resolves direction independently.
132	    <span className="wrap-anywhere block whitespace-pre-line" data-slot="aui_user-inline-text">
133	      {nodes.map((node, nodeIndex) =>
134	        node.kind === 'inline-code' ? (
135	          <code
136	            className="mx-px rounded bg-[color-mix(in_srgb,currentColor_8%,transparent)] px-1 py-px font-mono text-[0.92em]"
137	            data-slot="aui_user-inline-code"
138	            key={`code-${nodeIndex}`}
139	          >
140	            {node.code}
141	          </code>
142	        ) : (
143	          // Pass plain-text bits through DirectiveContent so @file:/@url: chips
144	          // still render. DirectiveContent already preserves whitespace.
145	          <Fragment key={`text-${nodeIndex}`}>
146	            <DirectiveContent text={node.text} />
147	          </Fragment>
148	        )
149	      )}
150	    </span>
151	  )
152	}
```

#### hermes_cli/cli_output.py — prompt(function)

```python
1	"""Shared CLI output helpers for Hermes CLI modules.
2	
3	Extracts the identical ``print_info/success/warning/error`` and ``prompt()``
4	functions previously duplicated across setup.py, tools_config.py,
5	mcp_config.py, and memory_setup.py.
6	"""
7	
8	from hermes_cli.colors import Colors, color
9	from hermes_cli.secret_prompt import masked_secret_prompt
10	
11	
12	# ─── Print Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
13	
14	
15	def print_info(text: str) -> None:
16	    """Print a dim informational message."""
17	    print(color(f"  {text}", Colors.DIM))
18	
19	
20	def print_success(text: str) -> None:
21	    """Print a green success message with ✓ prefix."""
22	    print(color(f"✓ {text}", Colors.GREEN))
23	
24	
25	def print_warning(text: str) -> None:
26	    """Print a yellow warning message with ⚠ prefix."""
27	    print(color(f"⚠ {text}", Colors.YELLOW))
28	
29	
30	def print_error(text: str) -> None:
31	    """Print a red error message with ✗ prefix."""
32	    print(color(f"✗ {text}", Colors.RED))
33	
34	
35	def print_header(text: str) -> None:
36	    """Print a bold yellow header."""
37	    print(color(f"\n  {text}", Colors.YELLOW))
38	
39	
40	# ─── Input Prompts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
41	
42	
43	def prompt(
44	    question: str,
45	    default: str | None = None,
46	    password: bool = False,
47	) -> str:
48	    """Prompt the user for input with optional default and password masking.
49	
50	    Replaces the four independent ``_prompt()`` / ``prompt()`` implementations
51	    in setup.py, tools_config.py, mcp_config.py, and memory_setup.py.
52	
53	    Returns the user's input (stripped), or *default* if the user presses Enter.
54	    Returns empty string on Ctrl-C or EOF.
55	    """
56	    suffix = f" [{default}]" if default else ""
57	    display = color(f"  {question}{suffix}: ", Colors.YELLOW)
58	
59	    try:
60	        if password:
61	            value = masked_secret_prompt(display)
62	        else:
63	            value = input(display)
64	        value = value.strip()
65	        return value if value else (default or "")
66	    except (KeyboardInterrupt, EOFError):
67	        print()
68	        return ""
69	
70	
71	def prompt_yes_no(question: str, default: bool = True) -> bool:
72	    """Prompt for a yes/no answer. Returns bool."""
73	    hint = "Y/n" if default else "y/N"
74	    answer = prompt(f"{question} ({hint})")
75	    if not answer:
76	        return default
77	    return answer.lower().startswith("y")
```

#### hermes_cli/setup.py — calls(calls), prompt(function)

```python
192	    print()
193	
194	
195	def prompt(question: str, default: str = None, password: bool = False) -> str:
196	    """Prompt for input with optional default."""
197	    if default:
198	        display = f"{question} [{default}]: "
199	    else:
200	        display = f"{question}: "
201	
202	    try:
203	        if password:
204	            value = masked_secret_prompt(color(display, Colors.YELLOW))
205	        else:
206	            value = input(color(display, Colors.YELLOW))
207	
208	        cleaned = _sanitize_pasted_input(value)
209	        return cleaned.strip() or default or ""
210	    except (KeyboardInterrupt, EOFError):
211	        print()
212	        sys.exit(1)
213	
214	
215	_BRACKETED_PASTE_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\x1b\[\s*200~|\x1b\[\s*201~")

#### acp_adapter/server.py — calls(calls), references(references), prompt(method), prompt(calls), instantiates(instantiates)

```python
1289	
1290	    # ---- Prompt (core) ------------------------------------------------------
1291	
1292	    async def prompt(
1293	        self,
1294	        prompt: list[
1295	            TextContentBlock
1296	            | ImageContentBlock
1297	            | AudioContentBlock
1298	            | ResourceContentBlock
1299	            | EmbeddedResourceContentBlock
1300	        ],
1301	        session_id: str,
1302	        **kwargs: Any,
1303	    ) -> PromptResponse:
1304	        """Run Hermes on the user's prompt and stream events back to the editor."""
1305	        state = self.session_manager.get_session(session_id)
1306	        if state is None:
1307	            logger.error("prompt: session %s not found", session_id)
1308	            return PromptResponse(stop_reason="refusal")
1309	
1310	        user_text = _extract_text(prompt).strip()
1311	        user_content = _content_blocks_to_openai_user_content(prompt)
1312	        text_only_prompt = all(isinstance(block, TextContentBlock) for block in prompt)
1313	        has_content = bool(user_text) or (
1314	            isinstance(user_content, list) and bool(user_content)
1315	        )
1316	        if not has_content:
1317	            return PromptResponse(stop_reason="end_turn")
1318	
1319	        # /steer on an idle session has no in-flight tool call to inject into.
1320	        # Rewrite it so the payload runs as a normal user prompt, matching the
1321	        # gateway's behavior (gateway/run.py ~L4898). Two sub-cases:
1322	        #   1. Zed-interrupt salvage — a prior prompt was cancelled by the
1323	        #      client right before /steer arrived; replay it with the steer
1324	        #      text attached as explicit correction/guidance so the user's
1325	        #      in-flight work isn't lost.
1326	        #   2. Plain idle — no prior work to salvage; just run the steer
1327	        #      payload as a regular prompt. Without this, _cmd_steer would
1328	        #      silently append to state.queued_prompts and respond with
1329	        #      "No active turn — queued for the next turn", which looks like
1330	        #      /queue even though the user never typed /queue.
1331	        if text_only_prompt and isinstance(user_content, str) and user_text.startswith("/steer"):
1332	            steer_text = user_text.split(maxsplit=1)[1].strip() if len(user_text.split(maxsplit=1)) > 1 else ""
1333	            interrupted_prompt = ""
1334	            rewrite_idle = False
1335	            with state.runtime_lock:
1336	                if not state.is_running and steer_text:
1337	                    if state.interrupted_prompt_text:
1338	                        interrupted_prompt = state.interrupted_prompt_text
1339	                        state.interrupted_prompt_text = ""
1340	                    else:
1341	                        rewrite_idle = True
1342	            if interrupted_prompt:
1343	                user_text = (
1344	                    f"{interrupted_prompt}\n\n"
1345	                    f"User correction/guidance after interrupt: {steer_text}"
1346	                )
1347	                user_content = user_text
1348	            elif rewrite_idle:
1349	                user_text = steer_text
1350	                user_content = steer_text
1351	
1352	        # Intercept slash commands — handle locally without calling the LLM.
1353	        # Slash commands are text-only; if the client included images/resources,
1354	        # send the whole multimodal prompt to the agent instead of treating it as
1355	        # an ACP command.
1356	        if text_only_prompt and isinstance(user_content, str) and user_text.startswith("/"):
1357	            response_text = self._handle_slash_command(user_text, state)
1358	            if response_text is not None:
1359	                if self._conn:
1360	                    update = acp.update_agent_message_text(response_text)
1361	                    await self._conn.session_update(session_id, update)
1362	                    await self._send_usage_update(state)
1363	                return PromptResponse(stop_reason="end_turn")
1364	
1365	        # If Zed sends another regular prompt while the same ACP session is
1366	        # still running, queue it instead of racing two AIAgent loops against
1367	        # the same state.history. /steer and /queue are handled above and can
1368	        # land immediately.
1369	        with state.runtime_lock:
1370	            if state.is_running:
1371	                queued_text = user_text or "[Image attachment]"
1372	                state.queued_prompts.append(queued_text)
1373	                depth = len(state.queued_prompts)
1374	                if self._conn:
1375	                    update = acp.update_agent_message_text(
1376	                        f"Queued for the next turn. ({depth} queued)"
1377	                    )
1378	                    await self._conn.session_update(session_id, update)
1379	                return PromptResponse(stop_reason="end_turn")
1380	            state.is_running = True
1381	            state.current_prompt_text = user_text or "[Image attachment]"
1382	
1383	        logger.info("Prompt on session %s: %s", session_id, user_text[:100])
1384	
1385	        conn = self._conn
1386	        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
1387	
1388	        if state.cancel_event:
1389	            state.cancel_event.clear()
1390	
1391	        tool_call_ids: dict[str, Deque[str]] = defaultdict(deque)
1392	        tool_call_meta: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
1393	        previous_approval_cb = None
1394	        edit_approval_requester = None
1395	
1396	        streamed_message = False
1397	
1398	        if conn:
1399	            tool_progress_cb = make_tool_progress_cb(
1400	                conn,
1401	                session_id,
1402	                loop,
1403	                tool_call_ids,
1404	                tool_call_meta,
1405	                edit_approval_policy_getter=lambda: self._edit_approval_policy_for_state(state),
1406	            )
1407	            reasoning_cb = make_thinking_cb(conn, session_id, loop)
1408	            step_cb = make_step_cb(conn, session_id, loop, tool_call_ids, tool_call_meta)
1409	            message_cb = make_message_cb(conn, session_id, loop)
1410	
1411	            def stream_delta_cb(text: str) -> None:
1412	                nonlocal streamed_message
1413	                if text:
1414	                    streamed_message = True
1415	                message_cb(text)
1416	
1417	            approval_cb = make_approval_callback(conn.request_permission, loop, session_id)
1418	            try:
1419	                from acp_adapter.edit_approval import make_acp_edit_approval_requester
1420	
1421	                edit_approval_requester = make_acp_edit_approval_requester(
1422	                    conn.request_permission,
1423	                    loop,
1424	                    session_id,
1425	                    auto_approve_getter=lambda: self._edit_approval_policy_for_state(state),
1426	                )
1427	            except Exception:
1428	                logger.debug("Could not create ACP edit approval requester", exc_info=True)
1429	        else:
1430	            tool_progress_cb = None
1431	            reasoning_cb = None
1432	            step_cb = None
1433	            stream_delta_cb = None
1434	            approval_cb = None
1435	
1436	        agent = state.agent
1437	        agent.tool_progress_callback = tool_progress_cb
1438	        # ACP thought panes should not receive Hermes' local kawaii waiting/status
1439	        # updates. Route provider/model reasoning deltas instead; if the provider
1440	        # emits no reasoning, Zed should not get a fake "thinking" accordion.
1441	        agent.thinking_callback = None
1442	        agent.reasoning_callback = reasoning_cb
1443	        agent.step_callback = step_cb
1444	        agent.stream_delta_callback = stream_delta_cb
1445	
1446	        # Approval callback is per-thread (thread-local, GHSA-qg5c-hvr5-hjgr).
1447	        # Set it INSIDE _run_agent so the TLS write happens in the executor
1448	        # thread — setting it here would write to the event-loop thread's TLS,
1449	        # not the executor's. Also set HERMES_INTERACTIVE so approval.py
1450	        # takes the CLI-interactive path (which calls the registered
1451	        # callback via prompt_dangerous_approval) instead of the
1452	        # non-interactive auto-approve branch (GHSA-96vc-wcxf-jjff).
1453	        # ACP's conn.request_permission maps cleanly to the interactive
1454	        # callback shape — not the gateway-queue HERMES_EXEC_ASK path,
1455	        # which requires a notify_cb registered in _gateway_notify_cbs.
1456	        previous_approval_cb = None
1457	        previous_interactive = None
1458	        edit_approval_token = None
1459	        previous_session_id = None
1460	
1461	        def _run_agent() -> dict:
1462	            nonlocal previous_approval_cb, previous_interactive, edit_approval_token, previous_session_id
1463	            # Bind HERMES_SESSION_KEY for this session so per-session caches
1464	            # (e.g. the interactive sudo password cache in tools.terminal_tool)
1465	            # scope to the ACP session rather than leaking across sessions
1466	            # that land on the same reused executor thread. This call runs
1467	            # inside a contextvars.copy_context() below, so the ContextVar
1468	            # write is isolated from other concurrent ACP sessions.
1469	            try:
1470	                from gateway.session_context import (
1471	                    clear_session_vars,
1472	                    set_session_vars,
1473	                )
1474	                session_tokens = set_session_vars(session_key=session_id)
1475	            except Exception:
1476	                session_tokens = None
1477	                clear_session_vars = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
1478	                logger.debug("Could not set ACP session context", exc_info=True)
1479	            if approval_cb:
1480	                try:
1481	                    from tools import terminal_tool as _terminal_tool
1482	                    previous_approval_cb = _terminal_tool._get_approval_callback()
1483	                    _terminal_tool.set_approval_callback(approval_cb)
1484	                except Exception:
1485	                    logger.debug("Could not set ACP approval callback", exc_info=True)
1486	            if edit_approval_requester:
1487	                try:
1488	                    from acp_adapter.edit_approval import set_edit_approval_requester
1489	
1490	                    edit_approval_token = set_edit_approval_requester(edit_approval_requester)
1491	                except Exception:
1492	                    logger.debug("Could not set ACP edit approval requester", exc_info=True)
1493	            # Signal to tools.approval that we have an interactive callback
1494	            # and the non-interactive auto-approve path must not fire.
1495	            previous_interactive = os.environ.get("HERMES_INTERACTIVE")
1496	            os.environ["HERMES_INTERACTIVE"] = "1"
1497	            # Propagate the originating ACP session id to tools that want to
1498	            # tag side-effects with it (e.g. ``kanban_create`` stamps it on
1499	            # the new task so clients can render a per-session board). Save
1500	            # and restore around the agent call so a re-used executor thread
1501	            # never leaks one session's id into the next session's tools.
1502	            previous_session_id = os.environ.get("HERMES_SESSION_ID")
1503	            os.environ["HERMES_SESSION_ID"] = session_id
1504	            try:
1505	                result = agent.run_conversation(
1506	                    user_message=user_content,
1507	                    conversation_history=state.history,
1508	                    task_id=session_id,
1509	                    persist_user_message=user_text or "[Image attachment]",
1510	                )
1511	                return result
1512	            except Exception as e:
1513	                logger.exception("Agent error in session %s", session_id)
1514	                return {"final_response": f"Error: {e}", "messages": state.history}
1515	            finally:
1516	                # Restore HERMES_INTERACTIVE.
1517	                if previous_interactive is None:
1518	                    os.environ.pop("HERMES_INTERACTIVE", None)
1519	                else:
1520	                    os.environ["HERMES_INTERACTIVE"] = previous_interactive
1521	                # Restore HERMES_SESSION_ID symmetrically.
1522	                if previous_session_id is None:
1523	                    os.environ.pop("HERMES_SESSION_ID", None)
1524	                else:
1525	                    os.environ["HERMES_SESSION_ID"] = previous_session_id
1526	                if approval_cb:
1527	                    try:
1528	                        from tools import terminal_tool as _terminal_tool
1529	                        _terminal_tool.set_approval_callback(previous_approval_cb)
1530	                    except Exception:
1531	                        logger.debug("Could not restore approval callback", exc_info=True)
1532	                if edit_approval_token is not None:
1533	                    try:
1534	                        from acp_adapter.edit_approval import reset_edit_approval_requester
1535	
1536	                        reset_edit_approval_requester(edit_approval_token)
1537	                    except Exception:
1538	                        logger.debug("Could not restore ACP edit approval requester", exc_info=True)
1539	                if session_tokens is not None and clear_session_vars is not None:
1540	                    try:
1541	                        clear_session_vars(session_tokens)
1542	                    except Exception:
1543	                        logger.debug("Could not clear ACP session context", exc_info=True)
1544	
1545	        try:
1546	            # Snapshot the internal Hermes DB session id before the turn so we
1547	            # can detect a compression-driven session rotation afterwards. The
1548	            # ACP `session_id` stays the stable client handle; agent.session_id
1549	            # is the live internal head that compression may rotate.
1550	            pre_turn_hermes_id = getattr(state.agent, "session_id", None)
1551	            # Wrap the executor call in a fresh copy of the current context so
1552	            # concurrent ACP sessions on the shared ThreadPoolExecutor don't
1553	            # stomp on each other's ContextVar writes (HERMES_SESSION_KEY in
1554	            # particular — used by the interactive sudo password cache scope).
1555	            ctx = contextvars.copy_context()
1556	            result = await loop.run_in_executor(_executor, ctx.run, _run_agent)
1557	        except Exception:
1558	            logger.exception("Executor error for session %s", session_id)
1559	            with state.runtime_lock:
1560	                state.is_running = False
1561	                state.current_prompt_text = ""
1562	            return PromptResponse(stop_reason="end_turn")
1563	
1564	        if result.get("messages"):
1565	            state.history = result["messages"]
1566	            # Persist updated history so sessions survive process restarts.
1567	            self.session_manager.save_session(session_id)
1568	
1569	        # Detect a compression-driven internal session rotation. If the agent's
1570	        # DB head moved during the turn, emit a session_info_update carrying
1571	        # _meta.hermes.sessionProvenance so ACP clients can render the boundary
1572	        # and keep old/new ids in lineage. The ACP session_id is unchanged.
1573	        post_turn_hermes_id = getattr(state.agent, "session_id", None)
1574	        if (
1575	            conn
1576	            and post_turn_hermes_id
1577	            and pre_turn_hermes_id
1578	            and post_turn_hermes_id != pre_turn_hermes_id
1579	        ):
1580	            try:
1581	                await self._send_session_info_update(
1582	                    session_id,
1583	                    current_hermes_session_id=post_turn_hermes_id,
1584	                    previous_hermes_session_id=pre_turn_hermes_id,
1585	                )
1586	            except Exception:
1587	                logger.debug(
1588	                    "Could not emit ACP provenance update after rotation for %s",
1589	                    session_id,
1590	                    exc_info=True,
1591	                )
1592	
1593	        final_response = result.get("final_response", "")
1594	        cancelled = bool(state.cancel_event and state.cancel_event.is_set())
1595	        interrupted = bool(result.get("interrupted")) or cancelled
1596	        # Hermes' local "waiting for model response" interrupt status is metadata,
1597	        # not assistant prose — clients get cancellation from stop_reason instead.
1598	        from agent.conversation_loop import INTERRUPT_WAITING_FOR_MODEL_PREFIX
1599	
1600	        suppress_interrupt_response = interrupted and final_response.startswith(
1601	            INTERRUPT_WAITING_FOR_MODEL_PREFIX
1602	        )
1603	        if final_response and not suppress_interrupt_response:
1604	            try:
1605	                from agent.title_generator import maybe_auto_title
1606	
1607	                def _notify_title_update(_title: str) -> None:
1608	                    if conn:
1609	                        loop.call_soon_threadsafe(
1610	                            asyncio.create_task,
1611	                            self._send_session_info_update(session_id),
1612	                        )
1613	
1614	                maybe_auto_title(
1615	                    self.session_manager._get_db(),
1616	                    session_id,
1617	                    user_text,
1618	                    final_response,
1619	                    state.history,
1620	                    title_callback=_notify_title_update,
1621	                )
1622	            except Exception:
1623	                logger.debug("Failed to auto-title ACP session %s", session_id, exc_info=True)
1624	        if (
1625	            final_response
1626	            and conn
1627	            and not suppress_interrupt_response
1628	            and (not streamed_message or result.get("response_transformed"))
1629	        ):
1630	            # Deliver the final response when streaming did not already send it,
1631	            # or when a plugin hook transformed the response after streaming
1632	            # finished (e.g. transform_llm_output) — otherwise the appended /
1633	            # rewritten text never reaches the client.
1634	            update = acp.update_agent_message_text(final_response)
1635	            await conn.session_update(session_id, update)

1638	        # calls can acquire the session. Queued turns are intentionally run as

#### gateway/run.py — calls(calls), start(method), references(references)

4924	            )
4925	        return scheduled
4926	
4927	    async def start(self) -> bool:
4928	        """
4929	        Start the gateway and all configured platform adapters.
4930	        
4931	        Returns True if at least one adapter connected successfully.
4932	        """
4933	        logger.info("Starting Hermes Gateway...")
4934	        try:
4935	            self._gateway_loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
4936	        except RuntimeError:
4937	            self._gateway_loop = None
4938	        logger.info("Session storage: %s", self.config.sessions_dir)
4939	
4940	        # Sanity-check that systemd's TimeoutStopSec covers our drain
4941	        # window.  When the user upgraded hermes-agent without re-running
4942	        # ``hermes setup``, their unit file may still encode the old
4943	        # default — in which case SIGKILL hits mid-drain and looks like
4944	        # a phantom kill in the journal.  Best-effort, never raises.
4945	        try:
4946	            from gateway.shutdown_forensics import check_systemd_timing_alignment
4947	            _alignment = check_systemd_timing_alignment(self._restart_drain_timeout)
4948	            if _alignment is not None and _alignment.get("mismatch"):
4949	                logger.warning(
4950	                    "Stale systemd unit detected: %s has TimeoutStopSec=%.0fs but "
4951	                    "drain_timeout=%.0fs (expected >=%.0fs). systemd may SIGKILL the "
4952	                    "gateway mid-drain. Run `hermes gateway service install --replace` "
4953	                    "to regenerate the unit, or shorten agent.restart_drain_timeout.",
4954	                    _alignment.get("unit", "(unknown)"),
4955	                    _alignment["timeout_stop_sec"],
4956	                    _alignment["drain_timeout"],
4957	                    _alignment["expected_min"],
4958	                )
4959	        except Exception as _e:
4960	            logger.debug("check_systemd_timing_alignment failed: %s", _e)
4961	        # Log the resolved max_iterations budget so operators can verify the
4962	        # config.yaml → env bridge did the right thing at a glance (instead
4963	        # of silently running at a stale .env value for weeks).
4964	        try:
4965	            _effective_max_iter = int(os.getenv("HERMES_MAX_ITERATIONS", "90"))
4966	            logger.info(
4967	                "Agent budget: max_iterations=%d (agent.max_turns from config.yaml, "
4968	                "or HERMES_MAX_ITERATIONS from .env, or default 90)",
4969	                _effective_max_iter,
4970	            )
4971	        except Exception:
4972	            pass
4973	        # Redaction status: ON by default (#17691). Surface a prominent
4974	        # warning if an operator has explicitly opted out so they don't
4975	        # forget the downgrade is active — the redactor snapshots its
4976	        # state at import time, so this log line is the source of truth
4977	        # for this process's lifetime.
4978	        try:
4979	            _redact_raw = os.getenv("HERMES_REDACT_SECRETS", "true")
4980	            _redact_on = _redact_raw.lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
4981	            if _redact_on:
4982	                logger.info(
4983	                    "Secret redaction: ENABLED (tool output, logs, and chat "
4984	                    "responses are scrubbed before delivery)"
4985	                )
4986	            else:
4987	                logger.warning(
4988	                    "Secret redaction: DISABLED (HERMES_REDACT_SECRETS=%s). "
4989	                    "API keys and tokens may appear verbatim in chat output, "
4990	                    "session JSONs, and logs. Set security.redact_secrets: true "
4991	                    "in config.yaml to re-enable.",
4992	                    _redact_raw,
4993	                )
4994	        except Exception:
4995	            pass
4996	        try:
4997	            from hermes_cli.profiles import get_active_profile_name
4998	            _profile = get_active_profile_name()
4999	            if _profile and _profile != "default":
5000	                logger.info("Active profile: %s", _profile)
5001	        except Exception:
5002	            pass
5003	        try:
5004	            from gateway.status import write_runtime_status
5005	            write_runtime_status(gateway_state="starting", exit_reason=None)
5006	        except Exception:
5007	            pass
5008	
5009	        # Log any active supply-chain security advisories. Operators see this
5010	        # in gateway.log and `hermes status` surfaces it; we do NOT block
5011	        # startup or surface it inline to user messages, since the gateway
5012	        # operator is the one who can act on it (uninstall the package,
5013	        # rotate credentials).  See hermes_cli/security_advisories.py.
5014	        try:
5015	            from hermes_cli.security_advisories import (
5016	                detect_compromised,
5017	                gateway_log_message,
5018	            )
5019	            _adv_hits = detect_compromised()
5020	            _adv_msg = gateway_log_message(_adv_hits)
5021	            if _adv_msg:
5022	                logger.warning("%s", _adv_msg)
5023	                logger.warning(
5024	                    "Run `hermes doctor` on the gateway host for full "
5025	                    "remediation steps."
5026	                )
5027	        except Exception:
5028	            logger.debug(
5029	                "security advisory check failed at gateway startup",
5030	                exc_info=True,
5031	            )
5032	        
5033	        # Warn if no user allowlists are configured and open access is not opted in
5034	        _builtin_allowed_vars = (
5035	            "TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS", "DISCORD_ALLOWED_USERS",
5036	            "WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS", "WHATSAPP_CLOUD_ALLOWED_USERS",
5037	            "SLACK_ALLOWED_USERS",
5038	            "SIGNAL_ALLOWED_USERS", "SIGNAL_GROUP_ALLOWED_USERS",
5039	            "TELEGRAM_GROUP_ALLOWED_USERS",
5040	            "TELEGRAM_GROUP_ALLOWED_CHATS",
5041	            "EMAIL_ALLOWED_USERS",
5042	            "SMS_ALLOWED_USERS", "MATTERMOST_ALLOWED_USERS",
5043	            "MATRIX_ALLOWED_USERS", "DINGTALK_ALLOWED_USERS",
5044	            "FEISHU_ALLOWED_USERS",
5045	            "WECOM_ALLOWED_USERS",
5046	            "WECOM_CALLBACK_ALLOWED_USERS",
5047	            "WEIXIN_ALLOWED_USERS",
5048	            "BLUEBUBBLES_ALLOWED_USERS",
5049	            "QQ_ALLOWED_USERS",
5050	            "YUANBAO_ALLOWED_USERS",
5051	            "GATEWAY_ALLOWED_USERS",
5052	        )
5053	        _builtin_allow_all_vars = (
5054	            "TELEGRAM_ALLOW_ALL_USERS", "DISCORD_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5055	            "WHATSAPP_ALLOW_ALL_USERS", "WHATSAPP_CLOUD_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5056	            "SLACK_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5057	            "SIGNAL_ALLOW_ALL_USERS", "EMAIL_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5058	            "SMS_ALLOW_ALL_USERS", "MATTERMOST_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5059	            "MATRIX_ALLOW_ALL_USERS", "DINGTALK_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5060	            "FEISHU_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5061	            "WECOM_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5062	            "WECOM_CALLBACK_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5063	            "WEIXIN_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5064	            "BLUEBUBBLES_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5065	            "QQ_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5066	            "YUANBAO_ALLOW_ALL_USERS",
5067	        )
5068	        # Also pick up plugin-registered platforms — each entry can declare
5069	        # its own allowed_users_env / allow_all_env, so the warning stays
5070	        # accurate as plugins like IRC come online.
5071	        _plugin_allowed_vars: tuple = ()
5072	        _plugin_allow_all_vars: tuple = ()
5073	        try:
5074	            from gateway.platform_registry import platform_registry
5075	            _plugin_allowed_vars = tuple(
5076	                e.allowed_users_env for e in platform_registry.plugin_entries()
5077	                if e.allowed_users_env
5078	            )
5079	            _plugin_allow_all_vars = tuple(
5080	                e.allow_all_env for e in platform_registry.plugin_entries()
5081	                if e.allow_all_env
5082	            )
5083	        except Exception:
5084	            pass
5085	        _any_allowlist = any(
5086	            os.getenv(v) for v in _builtin_allowed_vars + _plugin_allowed_vars
5087	        )
5088	        _allow_all = os.getenv("GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS", "").lower() in {"true", "1", "yes"} or any(
5089	            os.getenv(v, "").lower() in {"true", "1", "yes"}
5090	            for v in _builtin_allow_all_vars + _plugin_allow_all_vars
5091	        )
5092	        if not _any_allowlist and not _allow_all:
5093	            logger.warning(
5094	                "No user allowlists configured. All unauthorized users will be denied. "
5095	                "Set GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS=true in ~/.hermes/.env to allow open access, "
5096	                "or configure platform allowlists (e.g., TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS=your_id)."
5097	            )
5098	        
5099	        # Discover Python plugins before shell hooks so plugin block
5100	        # decisions take precedence in tie cases.  The CLI startup path
5101	        # does this via an explicit call in hermes_cli/main.py; the
5102	        # gateway lazily imports run_agent inside per-request handlers,
5103	        # so the discover_plugins() side-effect in model_tools.py is NOT
5104	        # guaranteed to have run by the time we reach this point.
5105	        try:
5106	            from hermes_cli.plugins import discover_plugins
5107	            discover_plugins()
5108	        except Exception:
5109	            logger.warning(
5110	                "plugin discovery failed at gateway startup", exc_info=True,
5111	            )
5112	
5113	        # Register the generic relay adapter when a connector relay URL is
5114	        # configured (GATEWAY_RELAY_URL / gateway.relay_url). No URL -> no-op, so
5115	        # direct/single-tenant deployments are unaffected. When configured, the
5116	        # adapter dials the connector over a WebSocket, negotiates its capability
5117	        # descriptor at handshake, and bridges inbound/outbound like any platform.
5118	        try:
5119	            from gateway.relay import (
5120	                register_relay_adapter,
5121	                relay_url,
5122	                self_provision_relay,
5123	            )
5124	
5125	            # Boot-time relay self-provision: resolve the agent's NAS token ->
5126	            # POST /relay/provision -> set GATEWAY_RELAY_* in os.environ BEFORE
5127	            # registration reads them. No-op when relay is unconfigured, a secret
5128	            # is already pinned, or no NAS token resolves (self-hosted, unenrolled).
5129	            # Never raises.
5130	            self_provision_relay()
5131	
5132	            if register_relay_adapter():
5133	                logger.info("relay adapter registered (connector at %s)", relay_url())
5134	        except Exception:
5135	            logger.warning(
5136	                "relay adapter registration failed at gateway startup", exc_info=True,
5137	            )
5138	
5139	        # Register declarative shell hooks from cli-config.yaml.  Gateway
5140	        # has no TTY, so consent has to come from one of the three opt-in
5141	        # channels (--accept-hooks on launch, HERMES_ACCEPT_HOOKS env var,
5142	        # or hooks_auto_accept: true in config.yaml).  We pass
5143	        # accept_hooks=False here and let register_from_config resolve
5144	        # the effective value from env + config itself — the CLI-side
5145	        # registration already honored --accept-hooks, and re-reading
5146	        # hooks_auto_accept here would just duplicate that lookup.
5147	        # Failures are logged but must never block gateway startup.
5148	        try:
5149	            from hermes_cli.config import load_config
5150	            from agent.shell_hooks import register_from_config
5151	            register_from_config(load_config(), accept_hooks=False)
5152	        except Exception:
5153	            logger.debug(
5154	                "shell-hook registration failed at gateway startup",
5155	                exc_info=True,
5156	            )
5157	
5158	        # Discover and load event hooks
5159	        self.hooks.discover_and_load()
5160	
5161	        
5162	        # Recover background processes from checkpoint (crash recovery)
5163	        try:
5164	            from tools.process_registry import process_registry
5165	            recovered = process_registry.recover_from_checkpoint()
5166	            if recovered:
5167	                logger.info("Recovered %s background process(es) from previous run", recovered)
5168	        except Exception as e:
5169	            logger.warning("Process checkpoint recovery: %s", e)
5170	
5171	        # Suspend sessions that were active when the gateway last exited.
5172	        # This prevents stuck sessions from being blindly resumed on restart,
5173	        # which can create an unrecoverable loop (#7536).  Suspended sessions
5174	        # auto-reset on the next incoming message, giving the user a clean start.
5175	        #
5176	        # SKIP suspension after a clean (graceful) shutdown — the previous
5177	        # process already drained active agents, so sessions aren't stuck.
5178	        # This prevents unwanted auto-resets after `hermes update`,
5179	        # `hermes gateway restart`, or `/restart`.
5180	        _clean_marker = _hermes_home / ".clean_shutdown"
5181	        if _clean_marker.exists():
5182	            logger.info("Previous gateway exited cleanly — skipping session suspension")
5183	            try:
5184	                _clean_marker.unlink()
5185	            except Exception:
5186	                pass
5187	        else:
5188	            try:
5189	                suspended = self.session_store.suspend_recently_active()
5190	                if suspended:
5191	                    logger.info("Marked %d in-flight session(s) as resumable from previous run", suspended)
5192	            except Exception as e:
5193	                logger.warning("Session suspension on startup failed: %s", e)
5194	
5195	        # Stuck-loop detection (#7536): if a session has been active across
5196	        # 3+ consecutive restarts, it's probably stuck in a loop (the same
5197	        # history keeps causing the agent to hang).  Auto-suspend it so the
5198	        # user gets a clean slate on the next message.
5199	        try:
5200	            stuck = self._suspend_stuck_loop_sessions()
5201	            if stuck:
5202	                logger.warning("Auto-suspended %d stuck-loop session(s)", stuck)
5203	        except Exception as e:
5204	            logger.debug("Stuck-loop detection failed: %s", e)
5205	
5206	        # Serialize startup restore against inbound dispatch.  Platform
5207	        # adapters can begin receiving messages as soon as they connect, but
5208	        # restart-interrupted sessions are not auto-resumed until all startup
5209	        # wiring below completes.  Queue inbound messages until the resume
5210	        # pass runs and every synthetic resume turn has finished.
5211	        self._startup_restore_in_progress = True
5212	        self._startup_restore_queue = []
5213	        self._startup_restore_tasks = []
5214	
5215	        connected_count = 0
5216	        enabled_platform_count = 0
5217	        startup_nonretryable_errors: list[str] = []
5218	        startup_retryable_errors: list[str] = []
5219	        
5220	        # Initialize and connect each configured platform
5221	        for platform, platform_config in self.config.platforms.items():
5222	            if not platform_config.enabled:
5223	                continue
5224	            enabled_platform_count += 1
5225	            
5226	            adapter = self._create_adapter(platform, platform_config)
5227	            if not adapter:
5228	                # Distinguish between missing builtin deps and missing plugin
5229	                _pval = platform.value
5230	                _builtin_names = {m.value for m in Platform.__members__.values()}
5231	                if _pval not in _builtin_names:
5232	                    logger.warning(
5233	                        "No adapter for '%s' — is the plugin installed? "
5234	                        "(platform is enabled in config.yaml but no plugin registered it)",
5235	                        _pval,
5236	                    )
5237	                else:
5238	                    logger.warning("No adapter available for %s", _pval)
5239	                continue
5240	            
5241	            # Set up message + fatal error handlers
5242	            adapter.set_message_handler(self._handle_message)
5243	            adapter.set_fatal_error_handler(self._handle_adapter_fatal_error)
5244	            adapter.set_session_store(self.session_store)
5245	            adapter.set_busy_session_handler(self._handle_active_session_busy_message)
5246	            adapter.set_topic_recovery_fn(self._recover_telegram_topic_thread_id)
5247	            adapter._busy_text_mode = self._busy_text_mode
5248	            
5249	            # Try to connect
5250	            logger.info("Connecting to %s...", platform.value)
5251	            self._update_platform_runtime_status(
5252	                platform.value,
5253	                platform_state="connecting",
5254	                error_code=None,
5255	                error_message=None,
5256	            )
5257	            try:
5258	                success = await self._connect_adapter_with_timeout(adapter, platform)
5259	                if success:
5260	                    self.adapters[platform] = adapter
5261	                    self._sync_voice_mode_state_to_adapter(adapter)
5262	                    connected_count += 1
5263	                    self._update_platform_runtime_status(
5264	                        platform.value,
5265	                        platform_state="connected",
5266	                        error_code=None,
5267	                        error_message=None,
5268	                    )
5269	                    logger.info("✓ %s connected", platform.value)
5270	                else:
5271	                    logger.warning("✗ %s failed to connect", platform.value)
5272	                    # Defensive cleanup: a failed connect() may have
5273	                    # allocated resources (aiohttp.ClientSession, poll
5274	                    # tasks, bridge subprocesses) before giving up.
5275	                    # Without this call, those resources are orphaned
5276	                    # and Python logs "Unclosed client session" at
5277	                    # process exit. Adapter disconnect() implementations
5278	                    # are expected to be idempotent and tolerate
5279	                    # partial-init state.
5280	                    await self._safe_adapter_disconnect(adapter, platform)
5281	                    if adapter.has_fatal_error:
5282	                        self._update_platform_runtime_status(
5283	                            platform.value,
5284	                            platform_state="retrying" if adapter.fatal_error_retryable else "fatal",
5285	                            error_code=adapter.fatal_error_code,
5286	                            error_message=adapter.fatal_error_message,
5287	                        )
5288	                        target = (
5289	                            startup_retryable_errors
5290	                            if adapter.fatal_error_retryable
5291	                            else startup_nonretryable_errors
5292	                        )
5293	                        target.append(
5294	                            f"{platform.value}: {adapter.fatal_error_message}"
5295	                        )
5296	                        # Queue for reconnection if the error is retryable
5297	                        if adapter.fatal_error_retryable:
5298	                            self._failed_platforms[platform] = {
5299	                                "config": platform_config,
5300	                                "attempts": 1,
5301	                                "next_retry": time.monotonic() + 30,
5302	                            }
5303	                    else:
5304	                        self._update_platform_runtime_status(
5305	                            platform.value,
5306	                            platform_state="retrying",
5307	                            error_code=None,
5308	                            error_message="failed to connect",
5309	                        )
5310	                        startup_retryable_errors.append(
5311	                            f"{platform.value}: failed to connect"
5312	                        )
5313	                        # No fatal error info means likely a transient issue — queue for retry
5314	                        self._failed_platforms[platform] = {
5315	                            "config": platform_config,
5316	                            "attempts": 1,
5317	                            "next_retry": time.monotonic() + 30,
5318	                        }
5319	            except Exception as e:
5320	                logger.error("✗ %s error: %s", platform.value, e)
5321	                # Same defensive cleanup path for exceptions — an adapter
5322	                # that raised mid-connect may still have a live
5323	                # aiohttp.ClientSession or child subprocess.
5324	                await self._safe_adapter_disconnect(adapter, platform)
5325	                self._update_platform_runtime_status(
5326	                    platform.value,
5327	                    platform_state="retrying",
5328	                    error_code=None,
5329	                    error_message=str(e),
5330	                )
5331	                startup_retryable_errors.append(f"{platform.value}: {e}")
5332	                # Unexpected exceptions are typically transient — queue for retry
5333	                self._failed_platforms[platform] = {
5334	                    "config": platform_config,
5335	                    "attempts": 1,
5336	                    "next_retry": time.monotonic() + 30,
5337	                }
5338	        
5339	        if connected_count == 0:
5340	            if startup_nonretryable_errors:
5341	                reason = "; ".join(startup_nonretryable_errors)
5342	                logger.error("Gateway hit a non-retryable startup conflict: %s", reason)
5343	                try:
5344	                    from gateway.status import write_runtime_status
5345	                    write_runtime_status(gateway_state="startup_failed", exit_reason=reason)
